metadata.description: Default location for all resources.
Name for backend service site. Should only contain letters, numbers and hyphens, length from 3 to 20 symbols.
Azure Cosmos DB Account name.
Azure Cosmos DB database name.
Azure KeyVault name.
Azure App Service Plan name.
Azure App Insights name.
Name of AFD endpoint for web client. Value is required.
Azure storage account name for webjobs (as backend for Azure Functions).
Azure storage account name for data blobs and queues.
Azure storage account SKU name for data blobs and queues.
Log analytics workspace name.
Microsoft Entra ID application, used for accessing safeexchange.
Microsoft Entra ID application secret. If not specified, client certificate 'AADClient--ClientCertificate' will be used instead.
Microsoft Entra Tenant ID used for safeexchange instance. Default value corresponds to 'customers' tenant, i.e. a tenant where it is possible to use personal Microsoft accounts.
Microsoft Entra ID application's audience URI, used for accessing safeexchange.

Comma-separated list of claim types, in preference order, that TokenHelper.GetUpn consults when resolving the caller's UPN. First non-empty claim wins. Defaults to the safe pair (raw 'upn' + ClaimTypes.Upn). Operators can restore the legacy fallback chain — e.g. 'upn,http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/ws/2005/05/identity/claims/upn,preferred_username,email,http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/ws/2005/05/identity/claims/emailaddress' — if a specific deployment requires it, but note that preferred_username / email are not stable or unique per AAD identity and reintroduce a permission-aliasing risk.
Name of the Virtual Network.
Address prefix for the VNet.
Name of the NAT Gateway.
Name of the NAT Gateway public IP address.

Name of the Cosmos DB private endpoint. Defaults to '<dbaccount>-pe' which works when the Cosmos and KV accounts have differentiated base names (e.g. <site>-db and <site>-kv). Override explicitly when both accounts share the same base name (e.g. legacy prd where Cosmos and KV are both named 'safeexchange-backend' and the default would collide with the KV PE).
Name of the Key Vault private endpoint. Defaults to '<kv>-pe'. See safeexchange_cosmosdb_pe_name for when to override.

Optional list of IP addresses or CIDR ranges allowed to reach the two resources that the ARM deploy itself has to talk to from the operator's machine: (1) Key Vault data plane — ARM writes every Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/secrets resource through the data plane, so the vault must be reachable from the deploying IP during 'az deployment group create'; (2) WebJobs Storage — where 'func azure functionapp publish' uploads the deployment zip. Cosmos DB and Data Storage stay fully behind their private endpoints regardless of this value because their ARM child resources (sqlDatabases, roleAssignments, blob containers, queues) are all management-plane only. Populate with the public IP(s) of the operator's machine, or a CIDR range. Leave empty to require deploys from inside the VNet (e.g. via a self-hosted runner or VPN) — but note that with an empty list, 'az deployment' will fail on the Key Vault secrets unless public access is temporarily opened. Example: ["203.0.113.42", "198.51.100.0/29"].

Network isolation mode for the WebJobs storage account. 'privateEndpoints' creates blob/queue/table/file private endpoints (and the table/file private DNS zones) and disables public network access. 'serviceEndpoint' keeps the storage account on its public DNS name but locks data-plane access to the snet-functionapp subnet via a Microsoft.Storage service endpoint + virtualNetworkRules entry, with networkAcls.defaultAction=Deny; the operator's deployer_ip_addresses still apply for 'func azure functionapp publish'.

Network isolation mode for the data storage account (cryptoblobs container + delayed-webhooks queue). 'privateEndpoints' creates blob + queue private endpoints and disables public network access. 'serviceEndpoint' keeps the account on its public DNS name but locks data-plane access to the snet-functionapp subnet via a Microsoft.Storage service endpoint + virtualNetworkRules entry, with networkAcls.defaultAction=Deny. Unlike WebJobs storage, the deployer's IP is never added to ipRules — the operator never writes to data storage from a laptop, only ARM management-plane operations touch it. Switching modes leaves the previous mode's resources orphaned in Azure (ARM ignores them); delete them manually post-deploy.

Whether to provision the NAT Gateway and its public IP for outbound internet from snet-functionapp. When true (default) the function app gets a deterministic outbound egress IP suitable for IP-allowlisting by webhook subscribers. When false, neither the NAT GW nor its PIP are created and snet-functionapp's natGateway property is null — the function app then has NO general outbound internet (defaultOutboundAccess stays false either way), only the resources reachable via the VNet (storage + Cosmos + KV via PE/serviceEndpoint). This breaks: webhook delivery to external URLs, Microsoft Graph calls, AAD JWKS rotation refresh, and App Insights ingestion. Switching this to false leaves the previous NAT GW and PIP orphaned in Azure (ARM ignores them); delete them manually post-deploy to actually save the ~$37/mo.
